title: agrostis exarata f. asperifolia
intro: Agrostis exarata f. asperifolia, also known as the rough-leaved bentgrass, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and prevent infection. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and acting as an antiseptic. Agrostis exarata f. asperifolia can be prepared into teas, infusions, and salves, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
